1.activity: Land preparation — plough field 2-3 times, level the soil, flood with 5cm water.
1.tip: Add farmyard manure (FYM) 5 tons/acre before final ploughing.

2.activity: Prepare nursery beds. Soak seeds 24hrs, incubate 48hrs. Sow pre-germinated seeds.
2.tip: Treat seeds with fungicide to prevent blast disease.

3.activity: Transplant 21-day-old seedlings at 20×15cm spacing. Maintain 2-3cm water.
3.tip: Plant 2-3 seedlings per hill for optimal tillering.

4.activity: Apply 1st dose of urea (1/3 of total N). Keep field flooded.
4.tip: Gap fill any dead hills within 7 days of transplanting.

5.activity: First weeding — manual or herbicide. Monitor for stem borer.
5.tip: Use cono-weeder between rows to save labour.

6.activity: Apply 2nd dose of urea. Check for brown plant hopper.
6.tip: Avoid excess nitrogen — it attracts pests.

7.activity: Second weeding. Apply potash if deficient. Maintain 5cm water.
7.tip: Install light traps to monitor insect populations.

8.activity: Maximum tillering stage. Scout for leaf folder and blast.
8.tip: Spray neem-based pesticide if pest threshold is crossed.

9.activity: Panicle initiation — critical water stage. Apply final urea dose.
9.tip: Do NOT drain field during this stage — yield loss risk.

10.activity: Flowering begins. Maintain consistent water level.
10.tip: Avoid pesticide spraying during flowering (harms pollinators).

11.activity: Grain formation. Monitor for neck blast and sheath blight.
11.tip: Apply fungicide if disease symptoms appear on flag leaf.

12.activity: Grain filling stage. Gradually reduce water.
12.tip: Potash application now improves grain weight.

13.tip: Check grain moisture — harvest at 20-22% moisture.

14.activity: Final field drying. Prepare harvesting equipment.

15.activity: Harvest when 80% of grains are golden-yellow.
15.tip: Harvest in morning to reduce shattering losses.

16.activity: Threshing, drying to 14% moisture, and storage.
16.tip: Store in moisture-proof bags. Use hermetic storage if available.

1.activity: Deep ploughing. Incorporate crop residue. Apply basal FYM.
1.tip: Soil test before planting — maize is a heavy feeder.

2.activity: Fine tilth preparation. Form ridges/furrows. Apply basal DAP + potash.
2.tip: Treat seeds with Thiram for seedling disease prevention.

3.activity: Sow seeds at 60×20cm spacing, 5cm depth. Use seed drill for uniformity.
3.tip: Sow with onset of monsoon for rainfed; irrigate if summer crop.

4.activity: Germination and emergence. Thin to 1 plant/hill if needed.
4.tip: Gap fill within 7-10 days after emergence.

5.activity: First weeding. Apply 1st top-dress of nitrogen (urea).
5.tip: Weed-free first 30 days is critical for yield.

6.activity: Knee-high stage. Earthing up around plants. Second weeding.
6.tip: Earthing up prevents lodging (falling) during wind/rain.

7.activity: Apply 2nd top-dress of nitrogen. Monitor for fall armyworm.
7.tip: Spray Emamectin Benzoate if armyworm visible in whorl.

8.tip: Water stress during tasseling reduces yield by 50%.

9.activity: Silking stage. Pollen shedding. Maintain soil moisture.
9.tip: Do NOT spray pesticides — protect pollination.

10.activity: Cob formation. Monitor for cob borer and ear rot.
10.tip: Remove and destroy infected cobs to prevent spread.

11.tip: Potash helps grain weight and quality.

12.activity: Grain drying on plant. Check husk colour (brown = ready).
12.tip: Harvest at 25% grain moisture for mechanical harvesting.

13.activity: Harvest cobs. Dry in sun for 3-4 days.
13.tip: Stack cobs vertically to improve air circulation.

14.activity: Shell, clean, and store at 12% moisture.
14.tip: Use airtight containers to prevent storage pests.

1.activity: Deep ploughing after previous crop harvest. Soil solarization.
1.tip: Black soil retains moisture — avoid over-irrigation.

2.activity: Apply FYM + basal NPK. Form ridges and furrows.
2.tip: Treat seed with Imidacloprid for sucking pest protection.

3.activity: Sow Bt cotton seeds at 90×60cm spacing.
3.tip: Plant refuge crop (non-Bt) at 20% area as per regulation.

4.activity: Germination. Gap filling. Light irrigation if dry.
4.tip: Avoid waterlogging — cotton roots are sensitive.

5.activity: Thinning to one plant/spot. First weeding.
5.tip: Interculture between rows using bullock-drawn or power weeder.

6.activity: Apply 1st urea top-dress. Monitor for jassids and aphids.
6.tip: Install yellow sticky traps for whitefly monitoring.

7.activity: Second weeding. Earthing up. Apply micronutrients (Zn, Fe).
7.tip: Foliar spray of 0.5% ZnSO4 improves boll retention.

8.activity: Pinching (topping) at 12-15 node stage. Monitor growth.
8.tip: Topping redirects energy from height to boll production.

9.activity: Apply 2nd nitrogen top-dress. Irrigate if rainless >10 days.
9.tip: Excess nitrogen causes rank growth and attracts pests.

10.activity: Square (flower bud) formation begins. Scout for bollworms.
10.tip: Use pheromone traps — 8/acre for bollworm monitoring.

11.activity: Flowering starts. Apply potash for boll development.
11.tip: Critical water requirement period — ensure moisture.

12.activity: Peak flowering. Monitor American bollworm closely.
12.tip: Spray neem oil or Bt-based biopesticide if needed.

13.activity: Late flowering. Remove and destroy damaged squares.
13.tip: Use hand-picking of larvae if infestation is moderate.

14.tip: Excess N at this stage causes boll shedding.

15.activity: Boll development. Monitor for pink bollworm.
15.tip: Keep field clean — destroy fallen bolls to break pest cycle.

16.tip: Stop irrigation 2-3 weeks before expected first picking.

17.activity: First bolls opening. Prepare for picking.
17.tip: Pick only fully opened bolls — avoid immature ones.

18.activity: Continue boll maturation. Watch for staining from rain.
18.tip: Cover harvested cotton — wet cotton grades lower.

19.activity: First picking — pick white, fully open bolls.
19.tip: Pick in dry weather. Sort by grade during picking.

20.tip: Avoid mixing good lint with stained or immature kapas.

21.activity: Third picking if needed. Final harvesting.
21.tip: Remove crop residue after harvest to reduce pest carryover.

22.activity: Clean field. Plan for next season crop rotation.
22.tip: Rotate with legumes (pulses) to restore soil nitrogen.

1.activity: Deep ploughing (30cm). Level the field. Apply FYM (10 tons/acre).
1.tip: Good land preparation is key — sugarcane stays 12+ months.

4.activity: Plant 3-budded setts in furrows at 90cm spacing. Apply basal fertilizer.
4.tip: Treat setts in fungicide solution for 15 minutes before planting.

6.activity: Germination starts. Light irrigation every 7 days.

10.activity: Tillering phase. Apply first nitrogen top-dress.
10.tip: Earthing up promotes more tillers and prevents lodging.

16.activity: Grand growth begins. Increase irrigation frequency.
16.tip: This phase uses 70% of total water requirement.

20.activity: Propping to prevent lodging. Apply potash.
20.tip: Tie canes in groups of 4-5 using dry leaves.

30.activity: Cane elongation continues. Monitor for top borer.

35.activity: Maturity starts. Reduce irrigation. Stop nitrogen.
35.tip: Withholding water increases sugar content in cane.

43.activity: Harvest when brix reading is 20+. Cut at ground level.
43.tip: Harvest early morning. Send to mill within 24 hours.

48.activity: Ratoon management — apply FYM and irrigate for next crop.
48.tip: Good ratoon management can yield 3 successive harvests.

1.activity: Plough field 2-3 times. Apply FYM and basal NPK.

3.activity: Sow seeds at 22.5cm row spacing. Irrigate immediately.
3.tip: Optimal sowing temp: 20-25°C. Late sowing reduces yield.

4.activity: First irrigation at crown root stage (21 days).
4.tip: This is the most critical irrigation — don't skip.

6.tip: Use herbicide before crop canopy closes.

9.activity: 2nd irrigation at jointing. Apply remaining nitrogen.
9.tip: Nitrogen after jointing goes to grain protein.

12.tip: Spray fungicide if yellow/brown rust appears.

15.activity: Grain filling. Final irrigation if needed.
15.tip: Terminal heat stress reduces grain weight.

17.activity: Harvest when grain moisture is 12-14%. Stalks turn golden.

18.tip: Clean and fumigate storage before filling.

1.activity: Plough and prepare seedbed. Apply FYM 2 tons/acre.
1.tip: Ragi tolerates poor soils but responds well to FYM.

3.activity: Transplant 25-day nursery seedlings or direct sow.

4.activity: Gap filling. First weeding at 20 days.
4.tip: Early weed removal is crucial — ragi is a slow starter.

6.activity: Apply urea top-dress. Second weeding at 40 days.
6.tip: Finger millet doesn't need heavy fertilization.

8.tip: One irrigation at this stage significantly boosts yield.

11.activity: Grain filling. Monitor for blast disease.
11.tip: Spray Tricyclazole if neck blast symptoms appear.

13.activity: Harvest when earheads bend and grains harden.

14.tip: Ragi stores very well — up to 10 years in dry conditions.

1.activity: Plough and harrow. Apply gypsum 200 kg/acre.
1.tip: Gypsum is essential for groundnut — provides calcium and sulphur.

3.activity: Sow kernels at 30×10cm. Treat seeds with Rhizobium + Trichoderma.
3.tip: Sow at 5cm depth. Inoculation boosts nitrogen fixation.

5.activity: First weeding at 20 days. Apply urea if poor growth.
5.tip: Groundnut fixes its own nitrogen — minimal urea needed.

8.activity: Flowering begins. Apply gypsum at flowering (top-dress).
8.tip: Gypsum at flowering dramatically improves pod filling.

10.activity: Pegging stage — pegs enter soil. Earthing up.
10.tip: Loose soil around plants helps pegs penetrate easily.

12.activity: Pod formation underground. Maintain soil moisture.
12.tip: Water stress now causes empty pods.

15.activity: Pods maturing. Check by sampling and cracking open.
15.tip: Inside of shell turns dark when mature.

17.activity: Harvest — uproot plants, stack inverted for sun drying.
17.tip: Harvest at 70-80% pod maturity for best quality.

18.activity: Strip pods, dry to 8% moisture, grade and store.
18.tip: Store in gunny bags in dry, ventilated space.

1.activity: Prepare nursery beds. Sow coffee seeds in sand beds.
1.tip: Use shade net nursery. Seeds take 45-60 days to germinate.

5.activity: Transplant 6-month seedlings to main field at 6×6ft spacing.
5.tip: Plant during monsoon onset. Provide temporary shade.

10.activity: Establish shade trees (Silver Oak/Dadap). Weed around plants.
10.tip: 50% shade is ideal for arabica; 30% for robusta.

20.activity: First pruning to shape. Apply FYM + NPK.
20.tip: Training young coffee plants determines future yield.

30.activity: Canopy spreading. Regular mulching. Borer monitoring.
30.tip: White stem borer: paint trunk with lime + copper sulphate.

41.activity: Berry formation after blossom showers. Apply potash.
41.tip: Coffee berries take 8-9 months to ripen after flowering.

49.activity: Selective picking of ripe red cherries. Process same day.
49.tip: Only pick fully ripe cherries — green ones reduce quality.

52.activity: Final picking. Prune handled branches. Prepare for next cycle.
52.tip: Wet processing produces better grade than dry processing.

1.tip: Dig pits 1-2 months before planting for weathering.

5.activity: Plant 10-12 month seedlings. Fill pit with topsoil + FYM + sand.
5.tip: Plant with onset of monsoon. Don't bury the collar region.

10.activity: Basin irrigation every 4 days. Weed regularly in basin.
10.tip: Young palms need consistent water — drought kills.

20.activity: Apply NPK in 2 splits. Mulch the basin with coconut husk.
20.tip: Green manure cover crop between palms improves soil.

30.activity: Intercrop with banana/pineapple for income while palm grows.
30.tip: First harvest only after 5-7 years — intercropping sustains income.

40.activity: Continue fertilization. Monitor for rhinoceros beetle.
40.tip: Place naphthalene balls in leaf axils to repel beetles.

52.activity: End of Year 1. Plant is established. Continue care routines.
52.tip: Patience is key — coconut is a 60+ year investment.

1.activity: Light ploughing. Apply FYM. No heavy nitrogen needed.
1.tip: Pulses fix their own nitrogen — excess N harms nodulation.

3.tip: Treat with Rhizobium culture for free nitrogen fixation.

4.tip: One weeding at 20 days is usually sufficient.

7.activity: Flowering begins. One irrigation if soil is dry.
7.tip: Excess water during flowering causes flower drop.

9.activity: Pod filling. Monitor for pod borer (Helicoverpa).
9.tip: Hand-pick larvae or spray neem if infestation is high.

11.activity: Pods dry on plant. Leaves turn yellow and drop.
11.tip: Harvest when 80% pods are dry and brown.

12.activity: Uproot, sun-dry, thresh, and clean seed.
12.tip: Store in airtight containers to prevent bruchid infestation.

1.activity: Prepare raised beds 1m wide. Incorporate FYM heavily.
1.tip: Ginger needs loose, well-drained soil — raised beds prevent rot.

3.activity: Cut seed rhizomes (25-50g pieces with 2 buds). Treat with Mancozeb.
3.tip: Shade-dry treated rhizomes for 3 days before planting.

5.activity: Sprouting begins (3-4 weeks after planting). Mulch with green leaves.
5.tip: First mulch at planting, second at 40 days, third at 90 days.

9.activity: Active growth. First weeding + earthing up. Apply 1st urea dose.
9.tip: Mulch heavily — ginger needs moist but not waterlogged soil.

14.activity: Rapid growth. 2nd top-dress. Monitor for shoot borer.
14.tip: Spray Dimethoate if shoot borer damage exceeds 5%.

20.activity: Rhizome development. Apply potash. Reduce irrigation slightly.
20.tip: Potash enhances rhizome size and oil content.

25.activity: Rhizome bulking. Leaves start yellowing at tips.
25.tip: Don't irrigate if leaves are yellowing — that's maturity signal.

30.activity: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and dry (8-9 months).
30.tip: For fresh ginger, harvest at 6 months. For dry ginger, wait full term.

32.activity: Dig carefully. Wash, dry, and store or process.
32.tip: For seed purposes, store rhizomes in pit with sand cover.

1.activity: Plough and prepare seedbed. Apply basal NPK.
1.tip: Sunflower performs well after a cereal crop rotation.

3.activity: Sow at 60×30cm spacing. Irrigate after sowing.
3.tip: Use hybrid seeds for better uniformity and yield.

5.activity: Thinning to one plant/spot. First weeding.
5.tip: Sunflower competes poorly with weeds initially.

7.tip: Boron deficiency is common — apply borax if needed.

8.activity: Star bud stage. Critical irrigation. Place bee colonies nearby.
8.tip: Bee pollination can increase seed set by 30-40%.

10.activity: Full bloom. Monitor for head rot in rainy weather.
10.tip: Ensure field drainage to prevent Sclerotinia head rot.

12.activity: Back of head turns brown. Seeds harden.
12.tip: Cover heads with cloth bags if bird damage is severe.

13.activity: Harvest when head is fully dry and seeds are black.
13.tip: Cut heads, sun-dry 2-3 days, then thresh.

14.activity: Clean, bag, and store seeds at 8% moisture.
14.tip: Don't delay harvest — seeds shatter easily when over-dry.

1.tip: Fill pits with topsoil + 10kg FYM + neem cake.

3.activity: Plant tissue culture or sucker plantlets. Irrigate immediately.

8.activity: Apply first NPK dose. Desuckering — remove all side suckers.
8.tip: Keep only 1 main plant per pit. Suckers steal nutrients.

16.tip: Banana is a heavy feeder — fertigation is ideal.

24.activity: Pseudostem fully grown. Apply potash for flowering.
24.tip: Potash improves fruit quality and bunch weight.

30.activity: Bunch emerges. Prop plant with bamboo support.
30.tip: Propping prevents toppling — bunches can weigh 25-40kg.

36.activity: Remove male bud (bell) after last hand opens.
36.tip: Removing bell redirects energy to fruit filling.

42.activity: Fingers filling. Apply bunch spray for cosmetic quality.
42.tip: Bunch cover with perforated polythene improves appearance.

46.activity: Harvest when fingers are 75% round and light green.
46.tip: Cut bunch with 30cm stalk. Handle with care — bruises reduce value.

48.activity: Ripen in chamber or ethylene. Remove and replant ratoon.
48.tip: Ratoon crop from first sucker gives harvest in 8-10 months.

1.activity: Prepare raised beds. Incorporate 10 tons FYM/acre.
1.tip: Turmeric exhausts soil — rotate with legumes after.

3.activity: Plant mother/finger rhizomes at 30×20cm. Mulch with green leaves.
3.tip: Treat rhizomes with Mancozeb + Quinalphos for 30 minutes.

6.activity: Sprouting. Apply first mulch layer with leaves/straw.
6.tip: Mulching is critical — 3 applications during crop cycle.

10.activity: Active growth. Apply 1st urea + potash. First weeding.

16.activity: Peak growth. 2nd mulching. Monitor for rhizome rot.
16.tip: Drench with Mancozeb if rot symptoms appear.

20.tip: Reduce irrigation — excess water causes rotting.

28.activity: Leaves yellowing and drying. Stop irrigation.
28.tip: This is the sign of maturity (7-9 months after planting).

31.activity: Dig rhizomes carefully. Wash and sort by size.
31.tip: Fingers and mothers are processed separately.

32.activity: Boil for 45 min, sun-dry 10-15 days, and polish.
32.tip: Polish by rubbing in drums for better marketplace value.

1.activity: Plough and harrow. Apply FYM and basal fertilizer.
1.tip: Sorghum is drought-tolerant but responds to good preparation.

3.activity: Sow at 45×15cm spacing. Use improved hybrid seeds.
3.tip: Seed rate: 3-4 kg/acre. Treat with Thiram.

5.activity: Thinning and first weeding. Apply urea top-dress.

7.activity: Second weeding. Monitor for shoot fly (rainy season crop).
7.tip: Carbofuran granules in whorl control shoot fly.

8.activity: Flag leaf and panicle emergence. Irrigate if dry.
8.tip: Water at boot stage significantly improves grain yield.

10.activity: Flowering and grain formation. Bird scaring if needed.
10.tip: Use scarecrows and reflective tapes for bird control.

12.activity: Grain hardening. Panicle bends with weight.
12.tip: Test grain hardness by pressing with thumbnail.

13.activity: Harvest earheads first, then cut stalks for fodder.
13.tip: Sorghum fodder is excellent livestock feed.

14.activity: Thresh, clean, and store at 12% moisture.
14.tip: Grain can be stored 2-3 years in good conditions.
